source: Metawee Srikummool et al. — Naresuan University, Thailand
publications: Srikummool et al. 2007, BMC Evolutionary Biology — Genetic variation in Northern Thailand Hill Tribes
Kutanan et al. 2021, Molecular Biology and Evolution — Reconstructing Human Genetic History of Mainland SE Asia
Srikummool et al. 2022, Scientific Reports — Forensic & genetic characterizations of diverse southern Thai populations

Karen.notes: Lowest mtDNA diversity due to matrilocal residence; highest Y-STR diversity. Strong gene flow with Thai/Lao populations.

Hmong.notes: Highest mtDNA diversity; patrilocal structure with high female migration. Distinct from Sino-Tibetan patrilocal groups.

Mien.notes: Similar pattern to Hmong; close genetic relationship. Patrilocal with Taoist cultural influence.

Lahu.notes: Matrilocal like Karen but slightly higher mtDNA diversity. High M haplogroup frequency.

Akha.notes: Lowest Y-STR diversity among patrilocal Sino-Tibetan groups. High D haplogroup (ancient lineage).

Lisu.notes: Similar to Akha; low Y-STR diversity from patrilocal residence. Close genetic distance to Akha.

Khamu.notes: Austroasiatic speakers; matrilocal structure. High R haplogroup. Close to Palaung and Lua.

Palaung.notes: Tea-growing people; matrilocal. Intermediate diversity. Close to Khamu genetically.

Tai Dam.notes: Tai-speaking; very close to Tai Lue (Fst=3). High O haplogroup frequency. Significant Thai/Lao admixture.

Tai Lue.notes: Nearly identical to Tai Dam; minimal genetic distance. Both show Tai-Kadai expansion signature.

Lua.notes: Indigenous to northern Thailand; matrilocal. High M haplogroup. Close to Khamu and Palaung.

Mani.notes: Negrito hunter-gatherer; extremely isolated. Basal mtDNA haplogroups (M21a, R21, M17a) indicate ancient lineage. High K Y-chromosome. Severe genetic drift.

Mlabri.notes: 'Spirits of the yellow leaves'; lowest genetic diversity of any Thai population. Small effective population size. High D Y-chromosome.

Moklen.notes: Sea nomad; moderate diversity due to coastal interactions. High R haplogroup (Austronesian signal). Mixed ancestry with Thai/Malay.

Urak Lawoi.notes: Sea nomad; highest R haplogroup frequency (45%). Distinct from mainland groups. Closer to Moklen but more isolated.

key_findings.matrilocal_pattern: Low mtDNA diversity, high Y-STR diversity — women stay, men move in
key_findings.patrilocal_pattern: High mtDNA diversity, low Y-STR diversity — men stay, women move in
key_findings.nomadic_pattern: Low diversity across all markers — genetic drift in small populations
